Elemental Maelstrom  

Description

1: Decrease HP when cast by 250
2: Decrease Hitpoints by 19 per tick. Max: 69 per tick

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 87 Skill: Evocation
Casting Time: 3 Recast Time: 1.5
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Resist: Magic
Range: 200 Location: Any
Time of Day: Any Push Back: 1
Deletable: No Dot Stacking: Yes
Reflectable: Yes Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: Yes Interruptable: Yes
Short Buff Box: No Target Type: Single
Spell Type: Detrimental Source: Live 12/03

Messages

Cast on you: Your skin tears as bolts of elemental power strike you.
Cast on other: Soandso 's skin shreds and tears as bolts of elemental power strike.
Effect Fades: The elemental storm departs.

Game Description

Surrounds your target in an elemental maelstrom, causing 250 damage initially, and between 19 and @2 damage every six seconds for 30 secs (5 ticks).

Items with this effect

  • Spell: Elemental Maelstrom

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Elemental Maelstrom
ClassLevel
MAG43
Expansion:

Duration:
5 ticks

  • Sold By (1)
  • Dropped By (41)

# May 27 2007 at 10:59 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1714954560127
Scholar
77 posts
This spell now has a tradeskill tag?
▲ Page top
also drops in Wakening Lands
QuoteReply
# May 06 2007 at 8:42 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591940063681
2 posts
I looted this today from Grand Vizier Pookalacha in the holgresh cave.
▲ Page top
elemtal maelstrom
QuoteReply
# Jul 27 2006 at 4:24 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591898863617
15 posts
Loguine's numbers are right, but shock of swords too early could get agro on you, this spell will deal the same damage out over time, making it so you pet holds agro, and you dont get your face crushed in.
▲ Page top
elemental maelstrom
QuoteReply
# Jul 27 2006 at 4:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591898863617
15 posts
this spell does 600 dmg or so, over time. Try casting your direct 600 spell and watch how fast you instantly have the mob hitting you, instead of pet. Cast this spell, pet holds agro, and you still do the same damage. Umm, anybody ever played a shaman before? Its called a dot, and it decreases your chance of hate.
▲ Page top
Can be usefull
QuoteReply
# Dec 05 2004 at 4:10 AM Rating: Decent
Loginue
16 posts
Your post was some time ago,but its the best place to put this other than making a new one. This could be usefull to some mages out there, hears a calulated form. Elemental Maelstrom w/o bracer Cost: 240 Damage:250dd 70 per tick(5) Total:600 Elemental Maelstrom with bracer(20%) Cost: 240 Damage:250dd 84 per tick(5) Total(possible):670 Now looking at the nuke you get at that level Shock of Swords Cost:250 Damage:580(44) to 600(48) as you can see ElementM cost slightly less and could produce more damage assuming no increased nuke damage is applied, even so Element has a DD so even that could increase(thats untested by me though). So all in all i beleive this could be usefull to a select few BUT wouldnt make it a priority.
▲ Page top
Can be usefull
QuoteReply
# Jul 27 2006 at 4:16 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591898863617
15 posts
Look kids, if you initially pull with a dd, then you stand the chance that you will move above your pet on the mobs hate list, this spell allows you to do the same damage, over time, so your pet keeps agro.
▲ Page top
if...
QuoteReply
# Feb 10 2004 at 1:48 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592256564851
3 posts
If u have the other 44 mage nuke That does up to 600 damage would u really need this???
▲ Page top
Can be bought on a vendor...
QuoteReply
# Feb 09 2003 at 1:55 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592760103637
2 posts
Just found out this spell can be bought for 7.875plat (with 130 CHA) off Galarono McHalorsos in the center building of the Bazaar
▲ Page top
RE: Can be bought on a vendor...
QuoteReply
# May 23 2003 at 9:08 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1594570761161
Guru
142 posts
More than likely someone sold it to the vendor because they could not sell it.

It is not a particularly useful or efficient spell except for maybe 2 or 3 levels, so many mages skip it.
▲ Page top
RE: Can be bought on a vendor...
QuoteReply
# May 25 2005 at 11:44 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591792948725
4 posts
I disagree with your post of usefullness. Many classes use dot/root combos. This spell let's us add to that damage without breaking the root.
▲ Page top
RE: Can be bought on a vendor...
QuoteReply
# Dec 18 2005 at 4:57 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591807131893
Scholar
74 posts
Well just wanted to add: Without breaking root... well if you cast a DD you have a chance at breaking a root, and this does have a 250 dd so whether you use this or the other you still have a chance at breaking it, so ehh use the main DD heh.
▲ Page top
RE: Can be bought on a vendor...
QuoteReply
# Jan 12 2006 at 9:29 PM Rating: Good
Kyrros
Sage
164 posts
Root related issues aside...

This spell, unresisted, does 560 - 595 damage for only 43 mana... this is before any focus effects are taken into account. That mana/damage ratio is better that ALL of the Mage class DD spells, and is only out performed by higher lever AE Rain spells. Wrath of the Elements, which is this spells's level 55 upgrade, has an even better ratio.

Sinc it is a DoT, you can also be blasting your target with other spells while its ticking away.

The only reason I see to not use this spells at any level would be aggro or if you're fighting MoBs that don't live long enough to last the full 30 seconds the DoT lasts. The aggro issues can be mitigated with a little common sense and self-control on the part of the caster :p
____________________________
Kyrros <Knights Of Insomnia> - Bristlebane
Guild Leader and Guild Historian
Human Veteran Ranger and Warder of Tunare for 70 80 85 90 seasons
